briefs.title: Dennis Back appointed Chief Election Officer for 2026 Burnaby election
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 general local election, scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026. The appointment is needed because the previous appointee, Samantha Pellizzari, is on leave and not expected to return before the election. Alix Nay and Josh Comia remain as Deputy Chief Election Officers.

key_dates.label: 2026 Burnaby general local election date

briefs.source_body: 7.1 APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER 1. CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: North Road BIA renewal blocked — petition sufficient
briefs.summary: Council will consider a staff report certifying that petitions against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw (Bylaw #14797) are sufficient. The petition period ran February 25 to March 30, 2026. Of 121 property owners notified, 66 submitted Notices of Opposition covering 65 folio numbers — meeting the 50% parcel and 50% assessed value thresholds required to block adoption. Staff recommend Council not proceed with the renewal bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision on April 14, 2026 — if Council follows the staff recommendation, the BIA levy will not continue beyond March 31, 2026.

key_dates.label: Council vote on North Road BIA renewal — April 14, 2026

briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body: 7.2 CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL 1. Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period. see Bylaw #14797 Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_ Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026 corporate maintenance: KPMG auditor, Dec 12 reference date
briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving routine annual corporate maintenance for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) for 2026. Items include: selecting December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date; appointing KPMG as auditor until the next AGM, with directors to set auditor pay; receiving BHA's financial statements for the period ending December 31, 2025; and appointing directors (five independent directors for two-year terms, three City directors and one independent director for three-year terms). Business will be transacted via unanimous shareholder resolution instead of holding an AGM.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date selected

briefs.source_body: 7.3 BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE MAINTENANCE MATTERS FOR 2026 1. BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA). BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2025 financials and 2026 budget up for council approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider receiving the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements and 2026 operating budget. The BHA reported a clean audit from KPMG, an accumulated surplus of $7.52 million, and $31.75 million in cash as of December 31, 2025. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to provide $2.00 million in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1, supporting BHA's housing projects at 10th Avenue/Newcombe Street and Marine Drive (Edmonds).

briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendments proposed
briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, Parks Regulation Bylaw, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to allow an e-bike share pilot program and address the provincial electric kick scooter pilot program. Key proposed rules include: shared e-bikes and e-scooters may only park in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; no person may ride a cycle, motor assisted cycle, or electric kick scooter on a sidewalk unless permitted by a traffic control device; electric kick scooters are banned on major road networks and arterial roads except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; and no person may use these devices in parks except in designated areas. The rules are not yet in effect — council has not voted on them.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and public hearing notice — you may want to comment if you operate a business that could be affected by e-bike or e-scooter parking or traffic rules.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM 1. Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Programs - 2026-04-14 .pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City. briefs.title: Burnaby must submit housing target progress report; 910 net new units completed
briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing submission of Burnaby's Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act. The report covers the first six-month reporting period (Sept 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026), during which 910 net new housing units were completed (1,097 completions minus 187 demolitions). The five-year target is 10,240 net new units by August 31, 2030, with annual cumulative targets of 1,536 (Year 1), 3,174 (Year 2), 5,069 (Year 3), 7,373 (Year 4), and 10,240 (Year 5). The decision is not final — it is up for approval at the April 14, 2026 council meeting.
briefs.action_required: No immediate action required; monitor future reporting deadlines if your development projects are in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Year 1b progress report deadline (Sept 1, 2025 – Aug 31, 2026) — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 2 progress report deadline (Sept 1, 2026 – Aug 31, 2027) — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 3 progress report deadline (Sept 1, 2027 – Aug 31, 2028) — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 4 progress report deadline (Sept 1, 2028 – Aug 31, 2029) — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 5 progress report deadline (Sept 1, 2029 – Aug 31, 2030) — Burnaby Housing Target Order

briefs.title: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 energy program
briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) program, a voluntary provincial program that tracks and reports energy use in large buildings. If approved, the city would share building energy data with the province to help identify ways to save energy and reduce emissions. The decision is not final — council will vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from you now. Watch for council's decision — if approved, building owners may need to prepare energy data for future reporting.

briefs.title: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain for Mintara restaurant
briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use specific land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure to service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, including the Mintara restaurant. The City is not requesting compensation for the right-of-way.

key_dates.label: Council decision on FortisBC right-of-way — Burnaby

briefs.industries: Natural Resources, Construction & Development, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M modular four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue. The three-storey, 5,319 sq ft building will include four residential units (one accessible) with private outdoor space. The project is supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council. Construction is expected to take 6–8 months, with a useful life of 50–60 years (up to 80 with maintenance). The city will retain ownership and lease the units.
briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — this is a city procurement decision. Watch for the council vote result.

key_dates.label: Council vote on modular housing contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The project includes installing 45 lineal meters of 1.8m x 1.8m box culvert, 40 lineal meters of DI watermain, and replacing Metro Vancouver sanitary main. Funding comes from the Canada Community Building Fund Reserve and DCC Drainage Reserve Fund.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. The project includes replacing 16 culverts between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road, including major replacements at Beecher Creek and Crab Apple Creek, plus final paving of the trail. Work is funded through the 2026-2030 Financial Plan with TransLink funding for the roads component.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for possible trail closures or detours during construction.

key_dates.label: Council vote on contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450K contract change for Rogers cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a $450,000 contract change to continue cellular voice and data services with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. on a month-to-month basis while the city transitions to a new provider. The change covers up to nine months and brings the total estimated contract value to $3,495,900. The city is finalizing a new contract for mobile devices and plans to be brought forward at a future meeting.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for HVAC maintenance at city buildings
briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving a $579,600 contract change (including tax) to Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC maintenance and repair services at various City-owned buildings. The change extends the existing contract through June 30, 2026, while the City goes back to market for competitive pricing. The total estimated contract value including this change is $5,412,750.
briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners; this is a City procurement decision.

key_dates.label: Council vote on HVAC contract change

briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing Cisco WebEx contact centre licence subscription
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract renewal for the Cisco WebEx Contact Center license subscription with Long View Systems Corporation for an estimated $394,942 including taxes. If approved, the renewal would run from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029, and is needed to maintain the City's Customer Service Centre phone system and support future call queue migrations.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Cisco WebEx contract renewal
New contract term begins if approved

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.67M fire protection contract renewal for city buildings
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. for fire protection systems installation, maintenance, and repair. The proposed three-year renewal runs July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, with an estimated total cost of $1,666,061 including taxes. Final payment will be based on actual services delivered at hourly rates. The original contract was awarded July 1, 2024 for $650,075, with one change adding $7,350 for additional sites.

key_dates.label: Council vote on fire protection contract renewal
Contract renewal takes effect

briefs.title: Council to approve Burnaby Lake Overpass plaque wording and design
briefs.summary: Council will vote on the exact wording and design for a commemorative plaque to be installed at the Burnaby Lake Overpass, which officially opens April 26, 2026. The plaque will recognize the project and its opening, listing the mayor, councillors, chief administrative officer, owner's engineer (McElhanney), and contractor (Jacob Bros Construction Inc.), and noting partnership funding from TransLink, the Government of Canada, and the Province of British Columbia.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass official opening and plaque installation

briefs.title: Burnaby to hire external reviewer of city financial practices
briefs.summary: Council will vote on a proposal to engage an external reviewer to assess the City's financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting. The review aims to safeguard assets and mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents, driven by a sharp decline in density bonus revenues and higher procurement costs.
briefs.source_body: 8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES 1. FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents. FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey, 215-unit rental rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider forwarding a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building with 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom) at 5035 Beresford Street, in the Royal Oak area. The site is currently zoned M4 Special Industrial District and is occupied by a printing shop. The proposal includes 1.9m of road dedication along Beresford Street, 574.74 m² of outdoor rooftop amenity space, 646.16 m² of indoor amenity space, 38 residential vehicle parking spaces (100% EV ready), 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 47 visitor bicycle spaces. This is the first step in the approval process — if Council votes to forward it, the application will go to First and Second Reading at a future meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if the rezoning advances, you may have a chance to provide input.

key_dates.label: Council vote to forward rezoning application — Burnaby

briefs.title: Mayor's Task Force economic growth update — final recommendations due June 23, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The Task Force has identified strategic priorities and is validating them through a business community survey and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13–April 2, 2026. Three additional workshops are scheduled April 16 to end of May 2026 to finalize recommendations, which will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026. No decisions or votes were taken at this meeting.

key_dates.label: Task Force final recommendations presented to Council

briefs.source_body: 9.1 UPDATE ON MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY ECONOMIC GROWTH AND RESILIENCE 1. Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To update members of Council on the progress of the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience 2026-04-1

briefs.title: Developer forfeits $1,363,500 security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
briefs.summary: Oxford Properties has elected to forfeit a $1,363,500 security deposit instead of building the public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court, as it was unable to secure the required Crown Land Tenure from the Province. The City will hold the forfeited funds and may use them for the pier or an alternative public amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby. The covenant will be discharged from title once the City receives the payment.
briefs.source_body: 9.2 AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY 1. Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction. Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw: $1.75M levy over 5 years
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw 2026. The bylaw would impose a local service tax on Class 5 (light industry) and Class 6 (business) properties in the area, raising up to $1,755,147.81 over five years (April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2031) for business promotion, beautification, and graffiti removal. Enough property owners have signed petitions against it, so the bylaw may not pass.
briefs.action_required: Contact your city councillor before the February 24 vote to express support or opposition.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw council vote
BIA term begins — levy takes effect

briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
